entry.title: Connecting to MariaDB with JDBC Adapter
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP Integration Suite's Java Database Connectivity (JDBC) adapter has been a powerful tool for connecting to various cloud and on-premise databases. The supported databases include DB2, Microsoft SQL Server, Oracle, PostgreSQL, SAP HANA, and SAP ASE Service. Now, the JDBC adapter has added support for MariaDB, further expanding its capabilities.

MariaDB Support

The JDBC adapter now enables connectivity to MariaDB Cloud, allowing seamless integration with SAP Integration Suite. Additionally, it supports connectivity to MariaDB on-premise databases, but with a specific requirement: the integration scenarios must be processed in an Edge Integration Cell runtime location.

Note that stored procedure execution is not supported for this database.

On-Premise Connectivity Considerations

If you intend to establish a connection with an on-premise MariaDB database, it's important to note that you must have EIC activated. Without EIC, you won't be able to connect to the on-premise MariaDB via the cloud connector.

Getting Started

Before you can start connecting to MariaDB using the JDBC adapter, there are a few prerequisites to take care of:

  1. Upload the necessary drivers
  2. Add the Data Source

For detailed instructions on these steps, refer to the documentation on Configure JDBC Drivers and Managing JDBC Data Sources.

MariaDB Database Details

Here are the key details for connecting to MariaDB using the JDBC adapter:

- Database Provider: MariaDB plc

- Infrastructure: On-Premise (via EIC only) and Cloud

- SAP BTP Environment: Cloud Foundry

- JDBC URL Pattern: `jdbc:mariadb://<host>:<port>/<database_name>:property1=value1;property2=value2;...`

- JDBC URL Example: `jdbc:mariadb://mymariadb:1234/TEST_1`

Conclusion

The addition of MariaDB support to SAP Integration Suite's JDBC adapter opens up new possibilities for integrating with this popular database. Whether you're using MariaDB Cloud or On-Premise (via EIC), you can now leverage the power of SAP Integration Suite to streamline your data integration processes.

Remember, while this adapter enables connectivity between Cloud Integration and a remote database system, SAP cannot provide advice on configuring the external system or offer support related to it.

Happy integrating!

entry.title: Kickstart SAP TechEd 2024 with Autonomous Finance and Maximize Your Business Efficiency
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 is here! As we kick off for this pivotal event in the SAP calendar, anticipation is building around the revolutionary talks and groundbreaking ideas set to be unveiled. For this year’s fully virtual TechEd, I’m excited to share the new SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) use case, Autonomous Finance.

 

What is Autonomous Finance?

So, what exactly is autonomous finance, and why is it becoming a game-changer in the world of oCFO? Gartner states that autonomous finance is a “finance function where processes and activities are partly governed and majority operated by self-learning software agents that optimize front-, middle- and back-office operations." Powered by technologies like AI, machine learning, and automation, it will affect all parts of the finance function under the office of the CFO. 

So why should businesses be excited about autonomous finance? Let's examine some of its compelling advantages.

  1. Autonomy in finance drastically reduces human intervention, which translates into significant cost reductions for organizations.
  2. It also speeds up operations and boosts productivity.
  3. AI-driven analytics can provide deep, actionable insights from financial data, empowering businesses to make smarter, strategic decisions.

Take a look at this infographic on the trusted path to autonomous finance. 

 

Customer Success Stories

Several SAP customers are already tapping into the potential of Autonomous Finance to propel their businesses forward:

  • Connect departments: Endress+Hauser significantly boosted overall efficiency and simplified operations by utilizing SAP Integration Suite’s tools to standardize their data sources. Thanks to the suite’s APIs and capabilities, Endress+Hauser's customers can now effortlessly place equipment orders directly from their ERP systems. This, in turn, automatically generates a production order within Endress Hauser’s ERP system and forwards it to the production facility. 
    • Aligned priority use case: Deliver Connected Experiences - Accelerate integration and increase productivity
  • Analyze: ARAG adopted SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud to create a unified and efficient analytics solution, enabling easy data accessibility. The combination of these technologies enabled ARAG to make all their data more accessible across different countries and teams, which paved the way for faster gaining of meaningful insights.
    • Aligned priority use case 1: Integrate hyperscaler data with SAP Datasphere - Deliver insights using live data from SAP and hyperscalers
    • Aligned priority use case 2: Accelerate Financial Planning and Analysis - Enhance SAP S/4HANA with planning and analysis
  • Automate processes: Orbico's IT team implemented SAP Build Process Automation to automate specific processes and tasks, thereby reducing project times and costs. The team launched over 40 process automation projects within a year, including a bot that automated invoice processing from PDFs into SAP S/4HANA, recognizing invoices from 20 vendors.
    • Aligned priority use case: Increase efficiency and accuracy while reducing costs - Automate processes to empower finance professionals
  • Embed intelligence: To Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Formula One Team, efficiency is key to success. Formula One has cost caps that limit each team’s spending on cars per season with severe penalties for overshoots, making financial management one of their biggest challenges. Mercedes-AMG PETRONAS Team will leverage SAP S/4HANA Finance and SAP Business AI to optimize resource management and cost forecasting.
    • Aligned priority use case: Enhance customer support using the power of generative AI - Improve operations and decision-making with the power of generative AI

 

How to get started

SAP Discovery Center is an incredible asset to any business. If you’re in search of ways to start deriving value with SAP Business Technology Platform for your business, this is the place to start. It empowers users to easily uncover, find, and embark on SAP Business Technology Platform use cases.

Why SAP Discovery Center?

  1. SAP Discovery Center offers a catalogue of hundreds of detailed and comprehensive missions. These missions cover numerous aspects of business to inspire you on your SAP BTP journey. For example, when you’re ready to embark on the Autonomous Finance journey, these Discovery Center Missions will help you to take each step and make it best suited to your business needs.
  2. It provides guidance on how to use the wide range of services offered by SAP. This step-by-step guidance means you can use SAP’s powerful suite of tools to the best of your abilities and utilize the Free Tier and/or Free Trial.
  3. It encourages learning and exploration. With SAP Discovery Center, you not only understand how a host of innovative technologies could potentially benefit your business but also how to implement them efficiently for the best results with the provided reference architecture.
  4. The collaborative feature of SAP Discovery Center is very important. Knowledge and experiences are shared, thus enhancing productivity and enabling synergy. You can see which existing SAP customers have already implemented the use case of your interest and connect with SAP experts if you have any questions on the Mission.

Using SAP Discovery Center means you’re leveraging not just a tool but a collaborative platform that guides innovation, encourages learning, and boosts efficiency. Make sure you're tuned in for the XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center session by Nis Boy Naeve and Ingo Deck from SAP Discovery Center team to learn more. 

 

What else can I do with SAP Business Technology Platform?

If you are interested in understanding further what SAP Business Technology Platform can do for your business, check out our comprehensive use case one-pager. The document is designed to provide a clear outline of how SAP BTP can be utilized to enhance your business operations, per your department. Grasping the use cases will allow your business to leverage SAP BTP effectively to drive business performance and inform your strategy, discover new potential, and maximize your technology investment with our SAP BTP use case one-pager. Don’t forget to also check out SAP Business Technology Platform use cases on the website.

You can read further on the SAP Business Technology Platform Priority Use Case One-Pager on this blog article.

 

Get ready for SAP TechEd 2024

I hope this got you excited about SAP TechEd 2024. Don't miss this opportunity to be part of a transformative event, and make sure to register for the keynote session with Muhammad Alam. Register now to secure your spot, and add the following recommended sessions to your agenda.

  • DT100 | Unlocking the intelligent enterprise: Cloud ERP innovations for the future
  • DA180 | Explore SAP Datasphere and SAP Analytics Cloud end to end
  • IN103 | Modernize and elevate to the cloud with SAP Integration Suite
  • XP206 | Achieve enterprise automation with SAP BTP solutions and SAP Signavio
  • AI194 | SAP Business AI and Joule: What’s in it for innovators? 
  • XP180 | Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center

 

We look forward to connecting with you!

entry.title: 🚀Fire of Innovations: Highlights from SAP TechEd 2024 📢
entry.content:
The SAP TechEd 2024 event, themed “Fire of Innovations,” has once again set the stage for groundbreaking advancements in enterprise technology. Held virtually on October 8-9, this year’s conference showcased a plethora of new tools, updates, and strategies designed to empower businesses and developers alike. Here’s a deep dive into some of the most exciting announcements and innovations from the event.

1. Joule: The AI Copilot Revolution

One of the standout highlights was the unveiling of new capabilities for Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot. Joule has been supercharged with collaborative AI agents that can perform complex, cross-disciplinary tasks. These agents are designed to break down silos within organizations, enhancing productivity and allowing human workers to focus on more creative and strategic activities.

For instance, in dispute management, autonomous AI agents can now analyze and resolve issues such as incorrect invoices or duplicate payments. In financial accounting, these agents streamline processes like bill payments and ledger updates, quickly addressing any inconsistencies.

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Enhancements

SAP continues to expand its Business Technology Platform (BTP), which is central to its cloud strategy. This year, SAP announced the expansion of BTP coverage through eight new SAP-managed data centers. Additionally, the SAP Discovery Center now offers a Quick-Account-Setup feature, enabling businesses to deploy specific use cases with just a click.

The integration of ABAP Cloud into SAP Build was another significant update. This integration allows developers to create and maintain ABAP Cloud projects directly from the SAP Build lobby, ensuring a seamless and efficient development process.

3. Innovations in SAP Build

SAP Build has been strengthened to support a “Clean Core” strategy, which is crucial for maintaining system consistency and upgradeability. The SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, introduced for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, helps both key users and developers create on-stack and side-by-side extensions in an embedded experience.

4. Harnessing the Power of Data with SAP Knowledge Graph

SAP introduced the SAP Knowledge Graph, a next-generation solution that connects SAP data with rich business context. This tool is poised to help developers unlock the full value of SAP data, driving more informed decision-making and innovation.

5. Global Reach and Accessibility

This year’s SAP TechEd was a truly global event, with a free two-day virtual format that included keynotes, technical sessions, and developer enablement activities. The event was designed to be accessible to a worldwide audience, ensuring that everyone could benefit from the latest SAP innovations.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 has once again demonstrated SAP’s commitment to driving innovation and empowering businesses with cutting-edge technology. From AI advancements with Joule to enhancements in SAP BTP and SAP Build, the “Fire of Innovations” theme truly encapsulates the transformative potential of these new tools and strategies. As businesses continue to navigate the complexities of the digital age, SAP’s innovations provide a robust foundation for growth and success.

Stay tuned for more updates and insights from SAP TechEd as we continue to explore the future of enterprise technology.

Feel free to share your thoughts or ask any questions about the event! What innovation are you most excited about?

entry.title: Implementing Message Level Security in SAP CPI: A Deep Dive into PGP Encryption and Decryption
entry.content:
Hello, dear readers!

In the first part, we will introduce the fundamentals of PGP key exchange and explore how the roles of signer and verifier work within this framework. This foundational knowledge will set the stage for our subsequent discussions.

The second blog will delve into practical aspects, covering the creation of PGP keys and strategies for monitoring and maintaining security within SAP CPI. Understanding these processes is essential for implementing robust security measures in your integration scenarios.

Finally, in the third part, We will explore an end-to-end implementation of PGP encryption and decryption in SAP CPI. By the conclusion of this series, you'll have a comprehensive understanding of how to effectively secure your data exchanges and ensure the integrity of your communications.

 

Part 1: Understanding PGP Key Exchange and the Roles of Signer and Verifier

Welcome to the first part of our blog series on Message Level Security with PGP! In this installment, we will briefly explore PGP key exchange and the essential roles of signer and verifier.

 1: Introduction to PGP

  • Definition: PGP (Pretty Good Privacy) provides encryption and authentication for secure data communication.
  • Goals: Ensure confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity.

     For more details Please go below Documentation Page

  • PGP ENCRYPTOR

    https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/define-pgp-encryptor?locale=zh-CNversion%3DCloud

  • PGP DECRYPTOR

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/define-pgp-decryptor?locale=zh-  CNversion%3DCloud

 2: How PGP Works

  • Key Pair: Each user has a public key (shared) and a private key (kept secret).
  • Encryption: Data is encrypted using the recipient's public key, ensuring only they can decrypt it.

 

3: PGP Key Exchange

 

  • Secure Distribution: Public keys are shared among users through secure channels.
  • Trust Models: Users validate each other’s keys within a web of trust.

4: Roles of Signer and Verifier

  • Signer: Creates a message and signs it with their private key, ensuring authenticity.
  • Verifier: Uses the sender’s public key to verify the signature, confirming the message’s integrity and origin.

 5.Common scenario

Scenario 1-  CPI to Partner

 

Scenario 2- Partner to CPI

 

 6: Benefits of PGP

  • Enhanced Security: Protects sensitive information.
  • Data Integrity: Confirms data has not been altered.
  • Authentication: Validates sender identity.

 

Conclusion

In this first part, we've covered the basics of PGP key exchange and the roles of signer and verifier. Stay tuned for Part 2, where we will discuss PGP key creation and strategies for maintaining security in SAP CPI!


Thank you for reading this blog post, and I hope you found it informative and helpful. 
Please comment your views and suggestion.

entry.title: SAP TechEd 2024 - Summary
entry.content:
SAP TechEd 2024 delivered an inspiring keynote with a strong focus on how technology drives real-world business innovations. This session brought to light the latest advances in AI, cloud solutions, and SAP's vision for the future of enterprise software.

The keynote opened with a thought-provoking introduction about the human brain, its complexities, and how innovation emerges from millions of connections forming new ideas. This metaphor seamlessly tied into SAP's philosophy of turning ideas into tangible solutions—where concepts become tools for solving real business problems.

Innovation at the Heart of SAP’s Strategy

One of the central themes was the growing importance of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in SAP’s offerings. AI is now deeply integrated into SAP’s cloud-driven portfolio, designed to simplify customers' lives and meet evolving business needs. Senior AI Scientists at SAP are working on harnessing AI to make business software smarter and more intuitive. The keynote also emphasized SAP's efforts to ensure that its software is configurable, extensible, and unified, providing a seamless suite-like experience across its platforms.

SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

A significant highlight was SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP), which has seen rapid growth in customer adoption. BTP is the cornerstone for SAP’s cloud and AI-driven future, empowering businesses with application development, automation, data analytics, and AI capabilities all within one unified platform. The keynote highlighted that over 27,000 live customers and 2,800 partners are leveraging SAP BTP to drive innovation.

The announcement of new BTP regions running on infrastructure from partners like AWS, Microsoft, and Google, along with SAP-managed data centers, shows SAP’s commitment to providing broader regional coverage and reliable service. This expansion aims to help businesses operate globally while maintaining efficiency, security, and compliance.

Enhancements in Application Development and Automation

SAP unveiled its Clean Core Strategy, which allows businesses to build custom extensions in a way that keeps the core ERP system maintainable and upgradeable. With SAP’s low-code environment, developers can create AI-powered applications with ease, while ABAP Cloud now supports both on-stack and side-by-side development, giving businesses more flexibility in their customizations.

One exciting feature introduced was the SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, a tool designed to streamline the process of creating custom fields and extensions, allowing users to enhance SAP applications with minimal coding.

SAP Build and GenAI

In keeping with the AI theme, SAP showcased the GenAI capabilities within SAP Build. With Joule, an AI assistant, developers can create complex data models, build extensions, and generate applications through natural language prompts. This AI-driven approach reduces manual coding and helps users integrate advanced functionalities like time series forecasting and predictive analytics into their business processes.

Data and Analytics Innovations

The keynote further highlighted innovations in data and analytics, specifically focusing on SAP HANA Cloud, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Datasphere. The HANA Cloud Vector Engine and Knowledge Graph Engine were introduced, both of which enable businesses to harness structured and unstructured data for more context-aware AI-driven decisions. These engines promise faster and more accurate data processing, particularly in scenarios involving large language models and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

Additionally, the keynote showcased how SAP Datasphere is being adopted by large enterprises like Hershey’s and Bosch for data cataloging, integration, and self-service analytics, empowering businesses to access trusted data with ease.

Integration Suite and AI-Driven Optimization

The SAP Integration Suite now offers a modern, AI-powered solution for real-time end-to-end business process integration. With thousands of pre-built integrations and connectors, businesses can seamlessly integrate SAP and third-party applications. SAP is also leveraging AI-generated recommendations to optimize custom scripts, further enhancing developer productivity and reducing memory consumption.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 demonstrated SAP’s unwavering commitment to making innovation accessible, impactful, and transformative. From the integration of advanced AI capabilities to the expansion of BTP, SAP is setting a new standard in enterprise software. Whether you are looking to enhance your application development, automate processes, or harness the power of data, SAP’s latest innovations offer powerful tools to bring your ideas to life.

As SAP continues to evolve, the future of enterprise technology is more connected, intelligent, and adaptable than ever before.

Here is the Keynote from SAP:

Comment below your thoughts on TechEd.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite - IMAP and SMTP connection to Gmail with OAuth2 Authorization Code
entry.content:
Background

I was revisiting Practical SAP Cloud Integration by @yeeloon-khoo on email handling section. I am getting error when trying to connect to IMAP and SMTP of my own mailbox from Integration Suite channel by using 'Authentication: Plain User/Password' option. Both Outlook and Gmail are returning a similar error like below:

IMAP

javax.mail.AuthenticationFailedException: [AUTHENTICATIONFAILED] Invalid credentials (Failure)

SMTP

javax.mail.AuthenticationFailedException: 535 5.7.139 Authentication unsuccessful, basic authentication is disabled.

What happened

I did some search and found out that in 2024, main email providers like Outlook, Gmail, Yahoo etc. are phasing out Basic Authentication method and enforcing OAuth for 3rd party connection. SAP Note states that SAP Cloud Integration Mail Adapter is supporting OAuth for Gmail. I believe similar case to Outlook & Yahoo too.

3512455 - OAuth Authentication supported in Mail Adapter for Google SMTP - SAP for Me

'... starting in autumn of 2024, users must use OAuth with third-party apps to access Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Contacts. OAuth is a more secure access method, no longer use a password for access... '

With this, it is no longer possible to connect from SAP Integration Suite to these mail servers with just Username (email ID) and Password. While 'Plain User/Password' authentication method is not the most secured option, I believe this is still very widely use method throughout the years. This is especially true for POC or test scenarios as it provides the most simple and straightforward use case.

I try to search on how we could quickly get the email connection to SMTP and IMAP works again for SAP Integration Suite. However, there seems to be no straightforward dummy guide specifically for SAP Integration Suite. Useful references are scattered throughout different sites and forums.

I did quite some trial and error and manage to get it works on Gmail. I am sharing the steps and hopefully it could be helpful to the community. I didn't try out Outlook or Yahoo, but I believe the steps to get it work could be similar.

** Edited - Link added at the end for Microsoft365 Mail configuration guide

** You might be seeing Integration Suite, Cloud Integration, Cloud Platform Integration, IS, CI, CPI throughout this article. But we all know it is referring to the same thing. Welcome to the ever-changing SAP world!!

Step-by-step How To

Gmail Section

Before starting, we need to have a working Gmail ID. For this showcase, I am utilizing an old email ID which I do not use for day-to-day work. This email ID does not have MFA setup. Logon to the Gmail ID and we are good to start now.

1. Setup Gmail API. Go through Google Cloud Console.

2. Create a New Project. Give the project a name. In my case, I named it as cpi-email-api

3. Once created, select the project, and search for Gmail API

4. Enable the Gmail API

5. Once the service is enabled, we will see following site. Click on the top left menu if we are not seeing APIs & Services page

6. Go to Oauth consent screen, select External -> Create

7. Give an App name, in my case sap-cpi-trial. Also fill up the email field, in this case I am using the same email ID logon in this Google Cloud Console service. Save and continue

8. Add or remove scopes -> filter by Gmail API -> select all 14 scopes -> Update. Take note of the first scope name, we will utilize this in authentication config in Integration Suite later.

* We probably just need the first scope https://mail.google.com/, but I just selected all in this showcase.

9. All scopes related to Gmail API is now included. Save and continue.

10. Add Test user email ID. This email ID will be configured under authentication in SAP Integration Suite later. In this case, I am using the same email ID logon in this Google Cloud Console service.

11. Summary screen is shown after OAuth consent setup completed.

12. Go to Credentials -> Create Credentials -> OAuth client ID

13. Application type -> Web application. Give it a name, in my case cpi-trial-gmail. Create.

14. OAuth client is created with Client ID and Client Secret. Note the Client ID and Client Secret, it will be use in Integration Suite authentication setup.

15. We are temporary done with Gmail setup now. We will move to Integration Suite setup.

 

Integration Suite Section

1. In Integration Suite, create Security Material.

2. Create OAuth2 Authorization Code.

3. Fill up details:

Name - IS-GMAIL -> can be any name

Provider - Generic

Authorization URL - https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth?access_type=offline&prompt=consent

Token Service URL - https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/token

Redirect URL - copy this URL for usage in next step

Client ID - from Gmail API Oauth Step 14 (Gmail Section)

Client Secret - from Gmail API Oauth Step 14 (Gmail Section)

Send As - Basic Authentication Header

Username - Gmail ID which configured as Test User at Step 10 (Gmail Section)

Scope - https://mail.google.com -> if scope is empty, the connection wouldn't work. We apply the first scope from Step 8 above (Gmail Section)

The Authorization URL and Token Service URL is retrieved from Google developers guide below:

https://developers.google.com/gmail/api/auth/web-server

Additional parameters ?access_type=offline&prompt=consent added to Authorization URL so that refresh_token would present, and the authentication could work.

4. After the object is created, we need to go to Google Cloud Console -> APIs & Services -> Credentials to maintain Authorized redirect URIs with the Redirect URL value generated from step above.

5. Back to Integration Suite, Authorize the OAuth2 object we just created.

Choose the correct Gmail ID configured for the Gmail API.

Continue, after acknowledging the warning.

Ensure the email ID shown is correct, Continue

Done and success.

Hit Refresh and the status is Deployed

6. Create Test iFlow - Read_Send_Email

Sender Channel Connection

Sender Channel Adapter - Mail (IMAP4)

Address - imap.gmail.com:993

Proxy Type – Internet

Protection – IMAPS

Authentication – OAuth2 Authorization Code

Credential Name - IS-GMAIL -> this is the object name we created under Security Material from step 3 above

Sender Channel Processing

Folder - /SendAPI -> I am creating a subfolder SendAPI in my Gmail to place the test email to be pick up

Other variables leave as default.

Receiver Channel Connection

Receiver Channel Adapter – Mail

Address - smtp.gmail.com:587

Proxy Type – Internet

Protection – STARTTLS Optional

Authentication – OAuth2 Authorization Code

Credential Name - IS-GMAIL -> this is the object name we created under Security Material from step 3 above

Receiver Channel Processing

From - the Gmail ID which configured as Test User at Step 10 (Gmail Section)

To - any email ID which receiving the email. In this case I am using the same email ID.

Subject - any subject is fine for testing purpose. In this case Test Gmail OAuth

Other variables leave as default.

7. Deploy the iFlow.

 

Testing Section

1. Logon to Gmail ID which is configured as Test User at Step 10 (Gmail Section). Create Labels -> SendAPI. This is subfolder /SendAPI we configured at sender channel. Place a test email into this subfolder.

2. With default setting, the sender mail channel poll every 10 seconds. The email will be picked up and sent.

3. In Integration Suite Monitoring, we can see the message processing of the email.

 

Conclusion

It is indeed a lot more steps required in setting up OAuth for Gmail connection. I hope this article could be helpful to anyone who has similar scenario. Thanks for reading.

 

For Microsoft365 Mail configuration, follow blog by @franz_forsthofer 

Cloud Integration - Connect to Microsoft 365 Mail ... - SAP Community

entry.title: New Pipeline Concept features: supporting custom header properties and dynamic configuration
entry.content:
We have recently shipped a new version 1.0.7 of the Pipeline Concept were we introduced two new features:

  • Support for custom header properties
  • Support for dynamic configuration parameters

The package can be accessed from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. If you have already copied the package to your workspace in your SAP Integration Suite tenant, you can simply run the update of the package to be able to use the latest features.

Let me briefly show you how the new features can be leveraged.

Custom header properties

It is now possible to add custom header properties to the message processing log of the generic integration flows to be able to search for logs based on any payload data. For this, you need to pass a list of name and value pairs to the generic integration flows using the header customHeaderProperties.

You can configure the header in either the scenario-specific inbound processing flow or the scenario-specific inbound conversion flow depending on your integration scenario.

In our case, we like to create three custom header properties. So, in a content modifier, we first define the required exchange properties based on the respective xpath expressions.

Then, we define the header customHeaderProperties where we separate name and value using colon, and separating the value pairs using pipe. In our case, we set it as follows:

productId:${property.productId}|category:${property.category}|purchaseOrder:${property.purchaseOrder}.

In the generic integration flows, a groovy script splits up the list of name and value pairs and adds the custom header properties accordingly.

Once we have successfully exchanged messages for this very scenario, let's enter the monitoring. Let me take the chance to show you another new enhancements which we have recently shipped and which was highly demanded by many of you: the message status overview. In the monitoring page, I click on the Message Status Overview tile.

Here, I get an overview of all messages processed within the given time frame grouped by different artifact types or other parameters like sender, receiver, message type. In our case, I have grouped by sender.

From here, I can then navigate to the message monitor. As you can see, you get the custom headers displayed in the Properties tab of the message processing logs of the generic integration flows. Furthermore, in the filter section you can search for specific custom header values.

Dynamic configuration parameters

You can pass any header starting with dc as dynamic configuration parameters to the generic integration flows. Next to the payload-based routing conditions, this allows you to support header-based routing conditions.

In the scenario-specific inbound processing flow, I can define such headers.

All generic integration flows allow those headers starting with dc.

In the XSLTs to determine the receivers or interfaces I can then use those headers to define my routing conditions.

If you like to try out the pipeline concept in general and the new features in particular, check out this github repository where I describe how to setup sample scenarios using the pipeline concept. Here, the dynamic configuration is used for scenario 1, the custom header properties have been maintained for scenarios 1, 3, 4, and 5.

entry.title: SAP Developer News: TechEd Day 2
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

SAP TechEd General

  • News Guide: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide.html
  • TechEd Watch Live: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html

Data and Analytics

  • Data and Analytics News: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/data-and-analytics.html
  • SAP HANA Cloud Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241009&search.offeringsproducts=1693018299239028TpZb

Integration

  • Integration News: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/news-guide/integration.html
  • Integration Sessions: https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog?tab.day=20241009&search.track=1692371026432005VcKa

New SAP Build Capabilities

  • SAP Build Extensibility Wizard: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-extensibility-wizard-for-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/ba-p/13867427
  • AD105 | Enhance your fusion team collaboration with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog/session/1721791179884001rYRe
  • AD200 | Extend Joule with SAP Build and develop custom enterprise skills https://www.sap.com/events/teched/virtual/flow/sap/te24/catalog/page/catalog/session/1722394882377001d2M9

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 TechEd Intro
1:13 Data and Analytics
2:25 Integration
3:49 New SAP Build Capabilities

Transcript

[Intro] It's a long way from Philly to Heidelberg, but the show must go on. We're late, aren't we? Yeah, we're late. We all know there's nothing like the real thing. Luckily, we've got our best people on the job. Oh, sorry, Josh! Josh, if you pulled your own weight, we wouldn't have to carry you through this place.

[Witalij] There were many extremely interesting announcements related to data and analytics during SAP TechEd Virtual. Some of them are coming in Q1 of 2025, while others are already available. For example, you might have noticed that as technology continues to evolve, we've also brought SAP Analytics Cloud to Apple Vision Pro. We also announced new data lake capabilities for SAP DataSphere. These new capabilities, including Object Store, Spark processing, and SQL on files, will be available starting in Q4 2024. Looking like I'll need to develop new CodeJam exercises and hit the road with them in 2025. I've done a lot of CodeJam focused on getting started with HANA ML capabilities, and I'm already excited that the development of intelligent data apps will become even more efficient with the introduction of the generative AI toolkit for SAP HANA Cloud. I hope you've seen this in the announcements.

Additionally, the new introduced Knowledge Graph engine in SAP HANA Cloud, combined with the accelerated vector engine, will be a powerful combo for building even more impactful AI-based applications using SAP data. So lots of work and fun ahead. Stay with us.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. Many announcements in the integration space as part of SAP TechEd 2024. So we have new integration adapters to easily connect to ANAPLAN, CUPA, HubSpot, NetSuite ERP, Snowflake, and more. We also have a new message status overview monitor. That's the aggregated overview of the status messages, for example, that we had in SAP Process Orchestration that we all love. The migration tool has been enhanced. Now it's possible to do mass migration of integration scenarios. There's a new licensing option for SAP Integration Suite, which is called Starter Edition. This is focused for grow with SAP customers and SAP SuccessFactors customers. Also, we have new generative AI capabilities in SAP Integration Suite. So we can now optimize our groovy scripts in SAP Cloud Integration. And finally, the product, SAP Event Broker for SAP Cloud Applications, was recently renamed to SAP Cloud Application Event Hub. Apart from that, you can check out the documentation in CAP to see how you can easily connect your CAP application deployed to SAP BTP to the SAP Application Event Hub.

[Shrini] SAP announces several improvements to SAP Build. Its comprehensive solution for extending S/4 HANA Cloud and other line of business applications. The most significant updates enhance SAP Build by streamlining S/4 HANA extension developments, introducing new generative AI capabilities, and expanding access to about development tools. A new extension wizard available now lets developers access SAP Build directly from S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition. With this access, developers can extend custom fields, business logic, and processes, either on-stack or side-by-side. A critical feature of this integration is the knowledge of business context, such as events and objects, when creating business processes and while transitioning between S/4 HANA and SAP brand. This preserves important business information, enabling developers to switch between environments seamlessly without losing crucial context.

In Q4 2024, the extension wizard will let developers create SAP Fiori and SAPUI5 applications, leveraging the full set of features available in SAP Build. In Q4 2024, new capabilities such as code exploration and documentation search in SAP Build Code will reduce development time for Java and JavaScript developers. Low-code developers and business users will also be able to create user interfaces and automate workflows with assistance from Juul. Joule is SAP's generative AI assistant. In Q1 2025, ABAP developers will be able to generate high-quality code and unit tests with Joule that comply with ABAP cloud development model. Joule will also be able to generate explanations for legacy code, making it easier to modernize legacy code bases and migrate to a clean code. By end of Q4 2024, Joule will also be integrated into SAP Build Work Zone, a low-code tool for creating engaging and personalized business sites. Joule's generative AI capabilities will offer user task automation and intelligent support.

[Sheena] By end of 2024, developers will also be able to use low-code capabilities in SAP build to enhance Joule with new skills, integrating it seamlessly with both SAP and non-SAP applications. This flexibility ensures Joule can be tailored to meet customer-specific business needs. Finally, developers can create applications that feature alternative AI capabilities by taking full advantage of HANA Cloud to ground large language models using its vector engine. Soon, developers will also be able to achieve this S/4 HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine, scheduled for general availability in the first half of 2025. By the end of 2024, SAP Build will include access to ABAP development tools and ABAP development environments for SAP S/4 HANA Cloud and SAP BTP. New access from the SAP Build Lobby, the solution's central starting point for all extension projects, will make it easier for multi-disciplinary development teams, also known as Fusion Teams, to build extensions and custom processes across all technology stacks. Developers will also be able to create and monitor their web cloud projects directly from SAP Build. This integration streamlines workflows by reducing the need to switch between different environments, significantly improving development efficiency.

entry.title: Procedure For End-To-End Encryption And Signing Of File Through SFTP Via Integration Suite PART-2
entry.content:
I have a requirement where I must process a payment file generated in SAP through integration suite by encrypting and signing the file, sending the file to the bank server. We are sending and receiving the payment file through SFTP, to connect to the bank server, we have provided them with a PUBLIC SSH KEY. We were given the public key of the receiver, folder path to place the file, server address and credentials of the same to login and we must process the file generated in SAP to the receiver by encrypting and signing the file. We should encrypt the data using receiver’s public key and sign the same using our private key. I have discussed the keys part in my first blog in this blog i would like to share the details regarding integration flow part, if any one have missed any details regarding the first part I am sharing the link here for your access to the first blog,

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/procedure-for-end-to-end-encryption-and-signing-of-file-through-sftp-via/ba-p/13873725

Once you have successfully uploaded the keys in PGP keys and after testing the connectivity of source and target destinations, we now have configured the sender and receiver configuration, I have developed my integration flow, please find below image,

 

I had captured the file which came from SAP in the content modifier and now I have the necessary data to be encrypted and signed, let us now see the configuration of PGPEncryptor, In PGPEncryptor please go to processing and there you can find the first option where is says signatures, as shown below,

 

 

  • I must encrypt the data and sign the same, so I am including the signatures.
  • You can select the content encryption algorithm, secret key length and compression algorithm according to your requirement, I had changed the compression algorithm to uncompressed as I don’t want to compress the data.
  • There is an option of armored, if you have enabled/ticked/checked the box then the resulting PGP message will be encoded with a specific header in base64.
  • You can check the integrity protected data packet if you want to create a symmetrically encrypted and integrity protected data packet.
  • Now you can find the option of encryption key user IDs from public keyring where you must add public key of the receiver which we have earlier uploaded in PGP Keys, also find the signer key user IDs where we must add our private/secret key which we have generated using GPA tool earlier and uploaded to the PGP keys, please find the image below,

 

 

Now that you have done with configuration of encryption and signing, let me also show you the configuration for decryption as shown below,

 

 

  • In PGPDecryptor there is no configuration for the decryption unless you want to verify the signature, where you receive the data which is encrypted and signed.
  • As you are receiver in the case of decryptor you require the public key of the sender to verify the signature of the sender.

I would like to share my point that this requirement is configured for host to host configuration with bank and customer.

So, I conclude the whole requirement where we have seen generating SSH KEYS using PUTTY KEY GENERATOR, generating KEY PAIR using GPA TOOL, processing the payment file from SAP to BANK server by encrypting and signing the file.

Please provide me your valuable feedback and if you any queries feel free to share.

entry.title: Generic Groovy script to log multiple custom headers in BTP Cloud integration iFlow runtime
entry.content:
SAP Cloud integration Apache camel framework provides generic method addCustomHeaderProperty to log custom header and there are lot of documentation around the same on how to log.

In this blog, we can see how we can use generic script to log any number of custom headers and use this method from integration flow based on specific interface needs.

 

Create generic script collection with below groovy script code:

logCustomHeader.groovy

Code:

def Message processData(Message message) {
    def count = 0;
    def list = message.getProperties().keySet();
    for( l in list ) {
        if (l.trim().toLowerCase().startsWith("loggingkey")) {
            count = count +1;
        }
    }
    if(count>0){
        for (i=1;i<=count;i++){
            def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message);
            if(messageLog != null){         messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty(message.getProperty("LoggingKey"+i),message.getProperty("LoggingValue"+i));
            }
        }
    }
    return message;
}

From integration flow, we can populate these values as exchange property (either from Content modifier or from code):

 

In this example, we can simply see that we used constants, but we could use xpath or other options in content modifier.

After properties are populated in some ways, we could call this global references groovy script:

 

In Cloud integration runtime monitoring, we can see these headers:

To conclude, this blog provides quick tip or generic common function which can be use to log any number of custom headers to be displayed on Cloud integration Runtime of iFlow.
